{"id":"qntm-63","title":"Gate v1: trusted broker execution for threshold-approved external actions","description":"Goal: move high-risk external API execution (e.g., wires) to a trusted broker model with threshold approval, exactly-once execution, and production-grade auditability.

Scope:
- Canonical intent + permit model
- Durable request/execution state
- Broker integration (no credential injection in gate)
- Policy + recipes model
- Security hardening and ops controls

Out of scope:
- Provider-specific UI polish
- Non-essential SDK ergonomics

Success criteria:
- 3-of-4 approvals can authorize one constrained action
- Action executes exactly once under concurrent triggers
- Secrets are never returned to callers","notes":"Mercury endpoint starter set for profile/recipe implementation:
- POST /api/v1/recipients (create recipient)
- POST /api/v1/account/{accountId}/request-send-money (queue payment requiring approval)
- GET /api/v1/request-send-money/{requestId} (check approval request status)
- POST /api/v1/account/{accountId}/transactions (direct send; high risk profile)
- GET /api/v1/account/{accountId}/transactions?requestId={requestId} (reconcile approved requests to transactions)
- Webhooks endpoints (event-driven reconciliation path)

Modeling note:
- Ship these as baseline service profiles with risk tiers and default threshold recommendations.
- Keep multisig as final authority via overlay policy; off-profile endpoints fail closed until explicitly approved.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T17:47:14Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-02-26T17:52:33Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"qntm-63.4","title":"Add broker integration API to gate (permit-based, no credential injection)","description":"Gate should stop constructing authenticated upstream requests directly.

Requirements:
- Gate emits validated ExecutionPermit to broker
- Gate passes idempotency key and correlation metadata
- Gate receives broker execution receipt and persists result
- mTLS or signed service-token auth between gate and broker

Acceptance:
- Integration test with fake broker verifies permit contract
- Gate never loads provider API key material","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T09:47:14Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-02-26T09:47:14Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"qntm-63.5","title":"Build trusted broker executor with secret-store-backed credentials","description":"Implement broker service that performs constrained execution while keeping secrets non-exportable.

- Credential templates support header/token injection at execution time
- Strict egress policy by service profile and path template

- Broker e2e tests show action succeeds without key exposure
- Secret rotation test passes without gate change","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T09:47:14Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-02-26T09:47:14Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"id":"qntm-63.6","title":"Policy model: shipped endpoint profiles + multisig-owned overlays + recipes","description":"Design policy control so multisig has final authority while defaults remain safe.

Direction:

- Allow org-specific overlay policies approved by multisig (e.g., 3-of-4)
- Recipes reference approved profiles and parameter schemas
- Any off-profile endpoint requires explicit multisig policy grant

Acceptance:
- Policy resolution order is deterministic and documented
- Recipe execution fails closed for unapproved endpoints
- Governance update flow for policy overlays is tested","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T09:47:15Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-02-26T09:47:15Z","work_type":"mutex"}

- Alerting hooks for failed/late/high-risk executions
- Break-glass runbook + emergency disable switch

{"id":"qntm-63.10","title":"Human e-sign integration for approval-required actions","description":"Integrate a human signature step into the threshold execution pipeline for actions that require wet-sign equivalent e-sign completion.

Requirements:
- New action mode: requires_human_signature=true in ExecutionIntent/policy profile
- Gate/broker creates signature request via provider API and stores envelope/request IDs
- Execution is blocked until provider webhook/status confirms completed signatures
- Signed artifact + audit evidence (timestamp, signer identity, doc hash, envelope ID) stored immutably
- Timeout/decline/cancel paths reflected in gate request status

Provider architecture:
- Provider interface abstraction in broker (DocuSign, Dropbox Sign adapters)
- Start with one default provider; keep API contract provider-agnostic
- Webhook signature verification required

Acceptance:
- Integration test: approved action enters waiting_signature then executes only after signed callback
- Decline/expire test: request transitions to terminal non-executed state
- Evidence record retrievable for audit","notes":"Starting implementation slice: provider interface + Dropbox Sign and DocuSign adapters with webhook verification and tests.

- Implemented Dropbox Sign adapter (template send, status poll, webhook verification)
- Implemented DocuSign adapter (template envelope create, status poll, webhook verification)
- Added adapter tests and docs (`docs/e-sign-providers.md`)

Remaining for this bead:

- Persist signature evidence and webhook-driven transitions in durable store","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-02-26T11:47:02Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-02-26T11:55:50Z","work_type":"mutex"}

{"design":"# Gateway as Conversation Participant

## Core Concept
Gateway is a qntm identity with its own Ed25519 keypair. It joins conversations as a participant and listens for gate messages by polling its dropbox — same as any user. The conversation IS the state. No separate org store, no dual-write.

## Key Changes from Current Design
- **Org goes away**. Conversation participants = signers. Threshold rules set via gate.config messages.
- **No HTTP message path**. Gateway polls dropbox, reads encrypted messages. Eliminates dual-write problem.
- **Secrets encrypted to gateway**. NaCl box (X25519) sealed to gateway's public key. Only gateway can decrypt. Other participants see the secret was provisioned but can't read the value.
- **Recipes have arguments**. path_params, query_params, body_schema. Client resolves templates before signing. What you approve is exactly what executes.
- **Pluggable**. Use qntm's default gateway or deploy your own. Just needs a keypair and access to the dropbox.

## New Message Types
- gate.promote — marks conversation as gate-enabled, sets initial threshold rules
- gate.secret — encrypted credential for gateway's vault
- gate.config — update threshold rules
- gate.request — (existing) now includes arguments map and recipe_name
- gate.approval — (existing) unchanged
- gate.executed — (existing) unchanged
- gate.result — API response body posted back to conversation

## Flows
1. **Promote**: Create conversation → invite gateway → send gate.promote with threshold rules
2. **Secret**: Encrypt credential to gateway pubkey → send gate.secret → gateway decrypts and vaults
3. **Request**: Pick recipe → fill arguments → client resolves templates → sign resolved URL → send gate.request
4. **Approve**: See full request with arguments/URL → click Approve → threshold met → gateway executes
5. **Execute**: Gateway sees threshold met → looks up credential in vault → makes API call → posts gate.executed + gate.result

## Signing
GateSignable replaces org_id with conversation_id. Signature covers resolved URL (not template), so approvers sign exactly what executes.

## Phases
Phase 1: Recipe arguments (extend Recipe struct, update starter.json, UI argument form)
Phase 2: Gateway identity + message loop (new gateway.go, polls dropbox, processes gate messages)
Phase 3: Promote flow (gate.promote message, conversation → gate-enabled)
Phase 4: Secret provisioning (NaCl box encryption, per-conversation vault)
Phase 5: Pluggable gateways (gateway init, gateway serve, documentation)","status":"in_progress"}

Needs sequence cursor tracking for incremental polls, local message history, and gate message rendering.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T12:51:45Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-11T12:51:45Z"}

{"id":"qntm-g11","title":"Ink TUI chat client for humans","description":"Ink/React terminal chat client sharing conceptual layout with AIM web UI. Separate from Python agent CLI - humans get their own identity and don't pollute agent keyspace. Uses @qntm/client TS library (identity, message, invite, gate, group, dropbox). Same component concepts as AIM UI rendered to terminal via Ink.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-11T12:51:45Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-11T15:11:20Z"}

{"id":"qntm-925","title":"Gateway vault: TTL-based secret expiry","description":"Secrets provisioned via gate.secret should have configurable TTLs (15min, 60min, 4hr) based on data classification/risk tier. After TTL expires, gateway purges decrypted secret from memory and vault. Requires human to re-provision. This limits prompt injection attack surface from 'always' to a bounded window. gate.secret message should include a ttl field. ConversationVault.Store should track expiry. Gateway polling loop should check and purge expired secrets.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:23:54Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:23:54Z"}

{"id":"qntm-qt2","title":"Gateway vault: prevent plaintext secret leakage","description":"Audit and harden: (1) Gateway must never log secret values. (2) gate.executed/gate.result messages must not include credential values. (3) Secrets must not appear in conversation history or memory. (4) ConversationVault should zero memory after use (best effort in GC languages). (5) Vault key should not be stored alongside encrypted data. Inspired by OpenClaw vault hardening practices.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:23:55Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:23:55Z"}

{"id":"qntm-t5o","title":"Gateway vault: secret revocation via gate.revoke message","description":"New gate.revoke message type lets any conversation participant revoke a specific secret by secret_id. Gateway processes revocation by purging from vault immediately. Provides emergency kill switch if a secret is compromised. Should also support revoking all secrets for a service.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:23:54Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:23:54Z"}

{"id":"qntm-ahx","title":"Gateway vault: human-controlled token refresh","description":"Gateway should NOT be able to autonomously refresh expired vault tokens. When a secret TTL expires, gateway should post a gate.expired notification to the conversation asking a human to re-provision. This keeps humans in the loop for security-sensitive operations. The gateway is deliberately limited - it can USE secrets but not CREATE or REFRESH them.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:23:55Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:23:55Z"}

new_value: Ink TUI scaffolded: App.tsx with identity management, conversation polling, keyboard nav, slash commands. Components: Sidebar, ChatPane, Composer, StatusBar, GateCard. Config in ~/.qntm-human/.
TTL-based secret expiry implemented: StoreWithTTL, expiry checking in Get, PurgeExpired, and ExpiresAt field in vault JSON. Tests passing.
Plaintext leakage audit complete: Scrub() zeroes values, vault encrypts at rest with AES-256-GCM, gateway checks expiry before use.
Secret revocation implemented: gate.revoke message type, handleRevoke in gateway, RevokePayload synced to Go/TS/Python.
Human-controlled token refresh: gate.expired message type with ExpiredPayload, checkExpiredCredentials in gateway loop, expiryNotified dedup. Gateway can USE but not CREATE/REFRESH secrets.

old_value: {"id":"qntm-tz6","title":"Gate-promoted conversations require unanimous consent for membership changes","description":"Once a conversation is promoted (gate-enabled), adding or removing members is a security-critical operation. Currently there are NO checks preventing member changes in promoted conversations. Implement unanimous agreement requirement: all current signers must approve any group_add or group_remove in a gate-promoted conversation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:53:33Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:53:33Z"}

{"id":"qntm-1ev","title":"Move gate UI from left sidebar to chat-specific right panel or tabs","description":"Gate functionality (recipes, promote, secrets) is currently in the left sidebar which is for master navigation. Since gate features are chat-specific, they should be in a tab under the chat or to the right of the chat pane.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:53:35Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-12T18:53:35Z"}

new_value: Unanimous consent for membership changes in promoted conversations: gate.membership_proposal/approval types, handleMembershipProposal/Approval handlers, direct group_add/remove rejected in promoted conversations. 8 tests.

Gate UI moved from left sidebar to right panel. Toggle button in chat header. Three-column layout when gate panel is open. Left sidebar now only has navigation: identities, invites, conversations, contacts.

old_value: {"id":"qntm-cds","title":"Rearchitect AIM UI as static site - eliminate Express server","description":"The AIM UI currently has a local Express server (server/index.mjs) that stores identity keys on disk and does all cryptography. This is wrong - the browser should import @qntm/client directly, do crypto in-browser, and store keys/state in localStorage. The Express server should be completely removed. This massively reduces security footprint by making AIM a static site that only talks to the remote dropbox.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:43:05Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:43:05Z"}

old_value: {"id":"qntm-vk5","title":"Fix AIM UI gate signer/public key serialization","description":"ui/aim-chat/src/qntm.ts stores conversation participants from the TS client conversation object, which are key IDs, then treats them as Ed25519 public keys in gatePromoteRequest and gateSecretRequest. The promote payload also serializes public_key as hex instead of base64url. This breaks gate promote/secret interop and auto-selected gateway keys. Add tests that cover promote and secret flows against the shared gateway expectations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T17:06:00Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-13T17:06:00Z"}

{"id":"qntm-szc","title":"Normalize gate.secret encrypted_blob encoding across clients and gateway","description":"python-dist/src/qntm/cli.py emits encrypted_blob as hex, ui/aim-chat/src/qntm.ts emits unpadded base64url, and python-dist/src/qntm/gateway.py decodes encrypted_blob with standard base64. Pick one wire encoding and add interop coverage so Python CLI, AIM UI, and gateway can all provision secrets successfully.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T17:06:00Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-13T17:06:00Z"}

{"id":"qntm-cnd","title":"Fix AIM UI gate.request payload hashing/schema mismatch","description":"In ui/aim-chat/src/qntm.ts, gateRunRequest hashes JSON.stringify(requestBody) before calling computePayloadHash and serializes request_body instead of the shared payload field. gateApproveRequest later hashes reqMsg.payload, so any request with a body can produce signatures/request hashes that do not match the Python client or gateway. Add regression tests for body-bearing recipes and approval hashing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T17:06:00Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-13T17:06:00Z"}

{"id":"qntm-5e1","title":"Threat-model AIM UI local key storage","description":"ui/aim-chat/src/store.ts persists identity private keys and conversation root/AEAD/nonce keys in localStorage as plaintext hex. For a hosted static app, any XSS or same-origin script compromise leaks long-lived identities and conversation secrets. Decide whether this is an accepted tradeoff or whether the UI should move to a safer storage model (for example WebCrypto non-exportable keys plus IndexedDB) and add browser hardening such as CSP.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"peter@vessenes.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T17:06:20Z","created_by":"PV","updated_at":"2026-03-13T17:06:20Z"}

new_value: Fixed gate.request payload hashing and payload field alignment in the AIM UI; added regression coverage.
Stored and learned participant public keys in the AIM UI, and normalized gate promote signer serialization to base64url public keys.
Normalized gate.secret encrypted_blob output to base64 across clients and made the gateway backward-compatible with legacy encodings.
Added Vitest coverage for the browser AIM UI adapter and API backup/recipe flows.
Updated the root and AIM UI docs to reflect the Python-first CLI and static browser UI architecture.
Upgraded the worker wrangler toolchain and cleared the npm audit advisories.
Documented the AIM UI local-storage trust model and added a restrictive CSP/referrer policy to harden the static app.
